title: UnionScan with partition table + keepOrder can't get correct result
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```
create table t(a int, b int, primary key(b)) PARTITION BY HASH(b) partitions 4;
analyze table t;
begin;
insert into t(a, b) values (6,6),(3,3),(9,9),(4,4),(5,5),(7,7),(8,8);
select * from t where b > 1 order by b limit 5;
```

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

```
mysql> select * from t where b > 1 order by b limit 5;
+------+---+
| a    | b |
+------+---+
|    3 | 3 |
|    4 | 4 |
|    5 | 5 |
|    6 | 6 |
|    7 | 7 |
+------+---+
5 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

```
mysql> select * from t where b > 1 order by b limit 5;
+------+---+
| a    | b |
+------+---+
|    4 | 4 |
|    8 | 8 |
|    5 | 5 |
|    9 | 9 |
|    6 | 6 |
+------+---+
5 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1(a INT, b INT, c VARCHAR(10), PRIMARY KEY(b));
INSERT INTO t1(a, b, c) VALUES (1, 1, 'one'), (2, 2, 'two'), (3, 3, 'three'), (4, 4, 'four'), (5, 5, 'five');
CREATE TABLE t2(d INT, e INT, f DATE, PRIMARY KEY(e));
SELECT * FROM t1 WHERE b > 1 ORDER BY b LIMIT 5;
key_bug_features: UnionScan with partition table and keepOrder
